entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The primary cereal crop discussed in the context of direct-seeding and transplanting.

entities.name: DIRECT-SEEDED RICE
entities.description: A rice cultivation method where seeds are sown directly into the field.

entities.name: PUDDLED TRANSPLANTED RICE
entities.description: A traditional rice cultivation method involving puddling and transplanting seedlings.

entities.name: WEEDS
entities.description: Unwanted plants that compete with rice for resources.

entities.name: WEED MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of controlling and limiting the growth of weeds in agricultural systems.

entities.name: PREVENTIVE APPROACHES
entities.description: Weed management strategies focusing on limiting the dispersal and persistence of weed propagules.

entities.name: CURATIVE TACTICS
entities.description: Weed management strategies used to treat existing weed infestations, such as herbicides.

entities.name: HERBICIDES
entities.description: Chemical substances used to kill or inhibit the growth of weeds.

entities.name: HERBICIDE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The inherited ability of a weed population to survive a herbicide dose that would normally be lethal.

entities.name: WEED SEEDBANK
entities.description: The reservoir of viable weed seeds present in the soil.

entities.name: WEED SEED PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process by which weeds produce seeds that contribute to the seedbank.

entities.name: SEED DISPERSAL
entities.description: The movement of seeds from one location to another via various agents.

entities.name: SEED PREDATION
entities.description: The consumption of weed seeds by organisms, reducing the seedbank.

entities.name: ZERO-TILLAGE
entities.description: A farming practice that eliminates plowing to minimize soil disturbance.

entities.name: FATAL GERMINATION
entities.description: The process where weed seeds germinate but die before establishing, often induced by specific conditions.

entities.name: STALE SEEDBED
entities.description: A technique where weeds are encouraged to germinate and then killed before the crop is sown.

entities.name: MULCHING
entities.description: The application of material to the soil surface to suppress weed growth.

entities.name: ANAEROBIC GERMINATION TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of rice cultivars to germinate and establish under anaerobic or flooded conditions.

entities.name: ANAEROBIC CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environments lacking oxygen, typically caused by flooding.

entities.name: FLOODING
entities.description: The application of water to saturate the soil, used for weed suppression.

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: The geographic region where direct-seeded rice is becoming popular.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area.

entities.name: CROP COMPETITIVENESS
entities.description: The ability of a crop to compete with weeds for resources.
